title: Annona neoamazonica
taxonomy: Annona neoamazonica belongs to the family Annonaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is part of the genus Annona, which includes other species such as Annona muricata and Annona squamosa. The plant is native to the Amazon region and is related to other tropical plants in the same family. Annona neoamazonica is a relatively newly discovered species, and more research is needed to fully understand its classification and characteristics. The plant's classification is likely to be further refined as more data becomes available.

Research suggests that it may have benefits in treating various health conditions, such as pain and inflammation. The plant is believed to contain annonaceous acetogenins, which have been reported to possess bioactive properties.
